Bilbo Baggins, a humble hobbit, is swept into an epic quest by the wizard Gandalf and a company of dwarves. Together, they journey across Middle-earth to reclaim the Lonely Mountain and its treasure from the dragon Smaug. Along the way, Bilbo discovers courage, wit, and a mysterious ring that will shape the fate of the world.

The first volume of The Lord of the Rings, this novel introduces Frodo Baggins, who inherits the One Ring. Tasked with its destruction, Frodo embarks on a perilous journey with the Fellowship—hobbits, men, a wizard, an elf, and a dwarf—facing dark forces that seek the Ring's power.

The second volume of The Lord of the Rings follows the breaking of the Fellowship. Aragorn, Legolas, and Gimli pursue the kidnapped hobbits; Merry and Pippin encounter the ancient Ents; Frodo and Sam, guided by Gollum, press onward toward Mordor to destroy the Ring.

The climactic conclusion of The Lord of the Rings. While armies clash in the War of the Ring, Frodo and Sam make their final push toward Mount Doom. The fate of Middle-earth hangs on their success—and the resilience of friendship against impossible odds.

A mythopoeic collection that lays the foundation of Tolkien’s legendarium. It recounts the creation of the world, the First Age, and the tragic tale of the Silmarils—jewels that inspired wars among Elves, Men, and the dark lord Morgoth. The work provides the deep history behind The Hobbit and The Lord of the Rings.

A collection of stories and essays expanding the lore of Middle-earth, edited by Christopher Tolkien. It includes tales of Númenor, the Istari (wizards), and extended accounts of characters like Túrin Turambar and Galadriel. The book enriches the depth of Tolkien’s world and connects the main narratives.
